title: Communist Party of India (Maoist) all its formations and front organisations as terrorist organisation

UW>K *TC "^Htft t t^U 4cfc)dl 8. am: sw M M ^ s fsJ^n-^rrq (Picii<«i) aqWnm, 1967 ^ r t*ra 35 ^r ^q-«rm ( i ) w w arfqr ^ n cms^Kt) cT«n ^ wft TOPF 3ik TPJ® ^ • ^ q ^ f T n t e ^ ^ ' ^ ^ T f 4,d^SKI 311^1 +<d11 3?k M v f ^ i Hfrm-^^Tq (fr^irq) 3#rtw?, 1967 ^T ^ H t ^ 4 ^ t ^!IT 33 cWT "3H^ #fl^rT Jjfeftd^T •^ MV^ic^ (HHfVlRsId sJ>q "CT^n 3lk Slftfldflf STcTi^rfqcT "34. ch^pH-^ "qrsf 3ifq> If^n oqrsitaKr) JG$ w% WK zfcnip#T3=f I'M [ q a u i-13014/4/2007-^q^-m] 2294 GI/2009 (1) -- 1 of 2 -- 2 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(h)] MINISTRY OF HOME AFFAIRS ORDER New Delhi, the 22nd June, 2009 S.0.1525(E).—Whereas the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967 (37 of 1967) (hereinafter referred to as the said Act) has been enacted to provide for more effective prevention of certain unlawful activities dealing with terrorist acts and matters connected therewith; 2. And whereas clause (m) of section 2 of the said Act defines the terrorist organisation to mean an organisation listed in the Schedule or organisations operating under the same name as an organisation so listed; 3. And whereas the Schedule to the said Act specify the list of terrorist organisations; 4. And whereas the Communist Party of India (Mamst-Leninist)-People's War, all its formations and front organisations have been specified at serial number 24 in the Schedule to the said Act and referred to in paragraph 3 above; 5. And whereas the Maoist Communist Centre (MCC), all its formations and front organisations have been specified as a terrorist organisation at serial number 25 to the Schedule to the said Act referred to in paragraph 3 above; 6. And whereas the Communist Party of India (Marxist-Leninist) and the Maoist Communist Centre referred to in the preceding paragraphs and its constitutes have merged together and formed the Communist Party of India (Maoist) in the year 2004; 7. And whereas the Central Government believes that the Communist Party of India (Maoist), referred in the preceding paragraph is involved in terrorism; 8. Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by sub-section (1) of section 35 of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967, the Central Government hereby makes an order so as to add that the Communist Party of India (Maoist) and all its formations and front organisations as terrorist organisation in the Schedule to the said Act and makes the following amendments for the said purpose, namely:— In the Schedule to the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967, after serial number 33 and entries relating thereto, the following serial number and entries shall be inserted, namely :— TERRORIST ORGANISATIONS "34. Communist Party of India (Maoist) all its formations and front organisations.". [F.No. 1-13014/4/2007-MN-III] Dr. KASHMIR SINGH, Jt. Secy. Printed by the Manager, Government of India Press, Ring Road, Mayapuri, New Delhi-l 10064 and Published by the Controller of Publications. Delhi-l 10054. -- 2 of 2 --
